principle.name: Test pyramid
principle.description: More unit tests, fewer integration tests, fewer e2e tests. The pyramid reflects cost and speed: unit tests are fast and cheap; e2e tests are slow and expensive.
principle.rationale: Inverting the pyramid leads to slow test suites and flaky builds. The pyramid optimizes for fast feedback and coverage.

principle.name: Realistic coverage targets
principle.description: Set coverage targets that are achievable and meaningful (e.g., 80% unit, 60% integration, critical flows e2e). Avoid 100% — it is rarely achievable and often counterproductive.
principle.rationale: 100% coverage encourages testing implementation details. Realistic targets focus on meaningful coverage.

principle.name: Test data strategy upfront
principle.description: Define how test data will be created, seeded, and isolated. Address fixtures, factories, and data cleanup.
principle.rationale: Test data issues cause flaky tests and slow debugging. Addressing it upfront prevents downstream pain.

principle.name: QA gates block deployment
principle.description: Define explicit gates: e.g., all e2e pass, coverage threshold met, no critical security findings. Deployment must not proceed when gates fail.
principle.rationale: Gates without enforcement are meaningless. Blocking deployment ensures quality.

principle.name: Regression strategy
principle.description: Define what runs on every commit, PR, and release. Balance speed vs coverage.
principle.rationale: Without a regression strategy, teams run ad-hoc tests or skip them. Defined strategy ensures consistency.

principle.name: Accessibility testing
principle.description: Include accessibility testing in the strategy: automated (aXe, Lighthouse) and manual (keyboard nav, screen reader).
principle.rationale: Accessibility is often overlooked. Including it in the strategy ensures it is tested.

pitfall.why_problematic: Deferring test strategy leads to ad-hoc testing, gaps, and technical debt. Tests never catch up.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define testing strategy during planning. Test design is part of planning, not implementation.

pitfall.why_problematic: Drives testing of implementation details, not behavior. Often unachievable and demoralizing.
pitfall.correct_approach: Set realistic targets (e.g., 80% unit, critical flows e2e). Focus on meaningful coverage.

pitfall.why_problematic: Unit tests alone miss integration bugs. Integration tests catch component interaction failures.
pitfall.correct_approach: Include integration layer in the pyramid. Map integration points to acceptance criteria.

pitfall.why_problematic: Critical user flows (auth, checkout, core features) may break without e2e coverage.
pitfall.correct_approach: Identify critical flows from PRD. Ensure each has at least one e2e test.

pitfall.why_problematic: Performance NFRs are often untested until production. Performance issues are discovered late.
pitfall.correct_approach: Include performance test plan when PRD or architecture specifies performance requirements.

pitfall.why_problematic: Tests fail due to data dependencies, flaky due to shared state, or are slow due to setup.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define fixtures, factories, seeding, and isolation for each test level.

pitfall.why_problematic: Tests pass locally but fail in CI. Environment differences cause confusion and slow feedback.
pitfall.correct_approach: Document environment requirements. Ensure test strategy works in both local and CI.

category.name: before_strategy_design
category.item: All plan artifacts read and acceptance criteria extracted.
Performance, security, and accessibility requirements identified.

category.name: strategy_content
category.item: Test pyramid defined with unit, integration, e2e.

category.name: before_completion
category.item: All acceptance criteria mapped to test types.
Performance, security, and accessibility test plans included.
